key_insight: A sparse random projection plus multinomial logistic regression model improved CNS tumor classification from DNA methylation data over a reference classifier.
clinical_relevance: Better tumor subtype assignment could support more reliable diagnostic decisions and downstream treatment selection in CNS cancers.
method: Reduced high-dimensional methylation features with Sparse Random Projection.
Classified tumors using multinomial logistic regression in a multiclass setting.
Compared performance against a widely used reference classifier under the same experimental setup.
evidence_signal: The model achieved 96% mean accuracy in stratified 3-fold cross-validation on 2,801 reference samples and 86% accuracy on 1,104 independent clinical samples, rising to 93% at the methylation family level.
watch_item: Performance is reported on specific cohorts and evaluation settings, so external validation and real-world deployment testing are still needed.
paper_title: A Novel Machine Learning Approach for Central Nervous System Tumor Classification from DNA Methylation
